title: String(bytes:encoding:.utf8) result silently stored as nil parameter
description: At line 522, String(bytes: fieldData, encoding: .utf8) is called on network-supplied bytes. If the bytes are not valid UTF-8, this returns nil. The nil value is then stored as a parameter via addParameter(field:value:) which accepts Any?. Downstream code calling string(forField:) will return nil, but code using the parameter dictionary directly or via lazy() may encounter unexpected nil values. More importantly, this means an attacker can inject invalid UTF-8 data to silently drop fields, potentially bypassing validation logic that checks for field presence.
vulnerable_code: self.addParameter(field: specField.name, value: String(bytes: fieldData, encoding: .utf8))
recommendation: Guard the UTF-8 conversion: if let str = String(bytes: fieldData, encoding: .utf8) { self.addParameter(field: specField.name, value: str) } else { Logger.error("Invalid UTF-8 in field \(specField.name)") }
